content.full_text:
California

Memorandum

To : Opinion Request Meeting Participants                    Date : April 13, 1978

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        John Giansello                                        78-141

Subject:

Discussion Item #1

Several Assemblymen who are candidates for re-election in 1978 form a committee to which they transfer funds from their own selection committees. The new committee is created to support former fellow Assemblymen who are candidates for federal office in 1978 and are not running for state office. At the time the money is transferred all parties understand that if the federal candidates are sufficiently successful at fundraising not all of the transferred funds will be needed. In that event the plan is to return the funds in the new committee's treasury to the sources from which it came.

Under these circumstances, must the state candidates report the new committee as a controlled committee? Must the new committee file state reports? Is the answer different if the new committee, in addition to the funds transferred to it from the state committees, also raises funds from the general public with the understanding that any portion not needed for federal candidates will go to the state candidates?

Discussion Item #2

A new store has opened. It makes its facilities available without charge to a political action committee for a fundraising fashion show. The store bears the cost of putting on the fashion show and also gives door prizes to the persons attending. The political action committee handles all of the fundraising aspects of the event. The store states it is acting for its own advertising purposes and, after the event publishes an advertisement in the local newspaper stating it does not support or oppose any candidate in the election. Has the store made a non-monetary contribution of any or all of the goods and services supplied? Is the advertising benefit the store receives full and adequate consideration for any expenses it incurs?

Discussion Item #1

Per opinion request meeting April 14, 1978, under the first set of
facts the federal entity is not a controlled committee nor need it
file state reports because it is not a committee at all in that
it is not raising or spending money to support a candidate as
defined in 82007. If the second set of facts arises the federal
entity will be a controlled committee and will have to file state
reports in that it is expressly raising money with the understand-
ing that some portion of the funds raised may be used to support
persons who are candidates within the meaning of 82007.


Discussion Item #2

Per opinion request meeting April 14, 1978, under the circumstances
of this case it was decided the store had not made a contribution.
Relevant factors were: (1) the committee took full responsibility
for bringing people to the fashion show and handled all advertising;
(2) the store, while this was a first-time incident for them and
they had no policy on the subject, did take out a 3 by 3 advertise-
ment in the local paper after the event stating it had not intended
to make a contribution but had merely made its facilities available
to a community group; (3) the models who displayed goods provided
by the stores were members of the committee; (4) persons attending
the show were informed the goods displayed were available for
purchase at the stores. We did not commit ourselves beyond the
decision that under the facts presented any payments made by the
stores were not made for political purposes. (The facts keep
changing on this one. When I called Jean Chacon back she said the
event was held at a restaurant and that the committee paid for the
hall. This change would not alter our advice, because under these
facts the stores are contributing less than before. All that is
donated here is the articles of clothing for use by the models in
the fashion show.)

sections.analysis:
Item #1

Per opinion request meeting April 14, 1978, under the first set of facts the federal entity is not a controlled committee nor need it file state reports because it is not a committee at all in that it is not raising or spending money to support a candidate as defined in 82007. If the second set of facts arises the federal entity will be a controlled committee and will have to file state reports in that it is expressly raising money with the understanding that some portion of the funds raised may be used to support persons who are candidates within the meaning of 82007.

Discussion Item #2

Per opinion request meeting April 14, 1978, under the circumstances of this case it was decided the store had not made a contribution. Relevant factors were: (1) the committee took full responsibility for bringing people to the fashion show and handled all advertising; (2) the store, while this was a first-time incident for them and they had no policy on the subject, did take out a 3 by 3 advertisement in the local paper after the event stating it had not intended to make a contribution but had merely made its facilities available to a community group; (3) the models who displayed goods provided by the stores were members of the committee; (4) persons attending the show were informed the goods displayed were available for purchase at the stores. We did not commit ourselves beyond the decision that under the facts presented any payments made by the stores were not made for political purposes. (The facts kept changing on this one. When I called Jean Chacon back she said the event was held at a restaurant and that the committee paid for the hall. This change would not alter our advice, because under these facts the stores are contributing less than before. All that is donated here is the articles of clothing for use by the models in the fashion show.)
